content.problemStatement:
In Chefland, there are 222 currencies in use, currency 111 and currency 222.  Normally, one unit of currency 111 is worth exactly 111 unit of currency 222.

Chef currently has AAA units of currency 111, and BBB units of currency 222.

However, he has found a glitch in the banking system, which allows him to trade XXX units of currency 111 for YYY units of currency 222, where X<YX < YX<Y, so this is a positive trade for him to make

Chef may make this trade multiple times, however it has to be exactly XXX units for YYY units and not just smaller parts in the same ratio - for example, if X=3X = 3X=3 and Y=6Y = 6Y=6 it is not allowed to exchange 222 units of currency 111 for 444 units of currency 222.

What is the maximum amount of money Chef can end up with? The amount of money being measured as the sum of currency 111 and 222.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line contains 444 integers - AAA, BBB, XXX and YYY.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum amount of money Chef can end up with.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : Chef can make the trade once, trading 666 units of currency 111 for 101010 units of currency 222. He ends up with 111 unit of currency 111, and 121212 units of currency 222 for a total of 131313.
Test Case 2 : Chef is unable to make any trades.
